QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_69

question.text: The Market-Wide Position Limit (MWPL) for futures and options on individual securities is:

answer.text: 20% of the number of shares held by non-promoters (free-float) in the underlying security
20% of the total shares outstanding including promoter holdings
10% of the free-float market capitalization in rupee terms
30% of the average daily trading volume in the cash market

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MWPL = 20% of free-float shares (non-promoter holdings). The CC specifies this on the last trading day of each month for the next month. Alert at 60% MWPL, ban on new positions at 95% MWPL, resume normal trading at 80%. **The Trap:** 20% is of free-float SHARES (number), not market cap (rupee value). Also, MWPL is set monthly, not daily. **Cross-Exam Context:** MWPL is a key regulatory concept across derivative exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** MWPL = 20% free-float shares. Alert at 60%, ban at 95%, resume at 80%.
